extra.reasoning: The step events apply acc := ((3*acc)+k) mod 48 (the triple-nested if-then-else subtracts 48 up to three times, which fully reduces mod 48 since the max pre-value 3*47+45=186 < 4*48=192). Constants: step_0 k=45, step_1 k=36, step_2 k=10. The faulty resume path t_resume_glitch fires send_attempt when (opted_out and acc==22), setting sent:=true after opt-out, which would violate globally(opted_out implies not sent). I ran a BFS over the 48-value acc space from initial acc=0 and found acc=22 is reachable (18 distinct accs reachable). Concrete path: from acc=0, step_1 gives (3*0+36)=36 (<48, stays 36); then step_2 gives (3*36+10)=118 -> 118-48=70 -> 70-48=22. So after [step_1, step_2], acc==22. Then optout sets opted_out=true, sent=false. Then send_attempt: the guard (opted_out and acc==22) holds, so t_resume_glitch sets sent:=true. The resulting reachable state has opted_out=true AND sent=true, violating the safety property. Sequence length is 4 events, well within the 50-event bound. Order matters: step_2 before step_1 from 0 yields acc=10 then 66 mod 48 = 18, not 22; the BFS-confirmed witness is step_1 then step_2.
